items.title: Iran Guards say strikes against Kuwait, Bahrain in retaliation for US attack
items.summary: TEHERAN, June 28, 2026 (AFP) - Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Sunday that it carried out strikes against Kuwait and Bahrain in retaliation for US attacks on Iranian territory, warning any further aggression would be met with a "crushing response." The Guards "destroyed eight important US military facilities at the Ali al-Salem base in Kuwait and at the Fifth Fleet naval base in Port Salman in Bahrain," they said in a statement. "Any enemy aggression, whatever the pretext, even against insignificant targets... will have a crushing response," the Guards added.

items.title: PN: We won't support PH, but backing BN in Johor polls is still undecided
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has yet to decide whether it will support Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor election for the 14 seats that will see straight fights between BN and Pakatan Harapan (PH). PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the coalition would discuss the matter before making a decision. He said PN component parties had yet to reach a collective decision on the matter.

items.title: FBM KLCI likely to trade within 1,660-1,690 range next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) is expected to trade within the 1,660-1,690 range next week, with volatility likely to persist as investors monitor developments in the global technology sector, geopolitical events and foreign fund flows, an analyst said. On a Friday-to-Friday basis, the June 2026 contract slid 48.0 points to 1,668.0, the July 2026 contract was 47.5 points easier at 1,669.50, the September 2026 contract shed 47.0 points to 1,650.0, and the December 2026 contract dipped 44 points to 1,653.0. Weekly turnover soared to 146,493 lots from 32,203 lots last week, while open interest surged to 93,884 contracts from 37,764 contracts in the preceding week.

items.title: Volkswagen Beetle believed destroyed in fire under Satok flyover
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — A classic car, believed to be a Volkswagen Beetle, parked beneath the Satok flyover here was destroyed in a fire early yesterday. “The fire was raging upon the firefighters’ arrival. “However, the car was completely destroyed,” said the Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) in a statement.

items.title: Uptrend seem on Bursa Malaysia next week on stock rotation factor
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Emas Index fell 316.03 points to 12,3654.02, the FBMT 100 Index lost 318.03 points to 12,201.59, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index sank 317.36 points to 12,241.30. Weekly turnover decreased to 15.54 billion units worth RM13.11 billion, from 16.92 billion units valued at RM14.62 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume narrowed to 7.78 billion units valued at RM11.58 billion against 9.73 billion units worth RM13.06 billion previously.

items.title: Sarawak Immigration detains 18 Indonesians in Kota Padawan joint operation
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 18 foreign nationals during a joint operation with the Padawan Municipal Council (MPP) around Mile 10, Kota Padawan, on Friday. “A total of 26 foreign nationals were checked to verify their travel documents and immigration status. “The operation resulted in the detention of 18 Indonesian nationals, comprising eight men and 10 women,” the statement said here yesterday.

items.title: Zahid to Johor Umno: Don't be disappointed if not chosen as candidates, your turn will come
items.summary: PONTIAN: Johor party leaders and members have been told to set aside any disappointment over not being selected as candidates for the state election. Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the party should focus on strengthening its machinery to ensure Barisan Nasional (BN) secures victory and maintains Johor as a BN and Umno stronghold. I am confident that opportunity will come one day," he said.

items.title: Tourism Ministry sharpens strategy, targets China, Indonesia, Japan, India and key long‑haul markets
items.summary: IPOH, June 28 — The Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC) through Tourism Malaysia, is strengthening its strategy by focusing on high-performing and stable markets including China, Indonesia, Japan and India. Deputy Minister Chiew Choon Man said this included focusing on long-haul markets such as Russia, Germany and Australia to ensure market diversification. He said the ministry would also continue to work closely with industry stakeholders to address operational challenges and cost pressures.

items.title: Quake jolts northeastern Japan, no tsunami warning issued
items.summary: The quake measured lower 5 on the Japanese seismic intensity scale of 7 in parts of Aomori and Iwate prefectures. There were also no abnormalities at a nuclear fuel reprocessing plant in Rokkasho, a Pacific coastal village in Aomori Prefecture, Japan Nuclear Fuel Ltd. said. Thursday's early-morning quake also occurred off Iwate at a depth of 44km, measuring upper 6 on the seismic intensity scale in Hashikami, Aomori.

items.title: Buy Malaysian Goods campaign rebranded as Jom Malaysia, says Armizan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Four decades after its introduction, the Buy Malaysian Goods campaign has been rebranded as Jom Beli Produk Malaysia (JOM MALAYSIA) to strengthen the branding of local products and services while fostering a spirit of economic patriotism among Malaysians. ““We want Jom Beli Produk Malaysia to become a national movement rooted in the values and spirit of patriotism, particularly economic patriotism,” he told reporters after launching the Jom Malaysia x Pesta Kita Festival at Central Market here yesterday. He said the initiative would adopt a whole-of-nation approach, involving collaboration among ministries, government agencies, industry players, traders and the public to comprehensively strengthen the use of locally made products.

items.title: Woman suffers 70pct burns from electric shock during cable theft attempt
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A woman suffered burns to 70 per cent of her body after receiving an electric shock while allegedly attempting to steal cables from a Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) substation in Taman Ria Jaya yesterday afternoon. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said she was rushed to hospital and placed in a medically induced coma. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Explosions in Kyiv, targeted by missile attack
items.summary: KYIV, (Ukraine): A ballistic missile attack is underway in Kyiv, the Ukrainian military said early today. ", the capital's mayor Vitali Klitschko said on Telegram. Russian strikes a day earlier in Dnipropetrovsk in central-eastern Ukraine and the northern Sumy region killed two people.

items.title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Saturday nominated a former police officer from Oklahoma to be the next director of the controversial US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, or ICE. Trump said Lance Schroyer was a former state trooper and United States Marine "with DECADES of experience locking up the worst of the worst." Schroyer would succeed Todd Lyons, appointed acting director of ICE in March 2025.

items.title: Iran says US violated peace deal as both sides attack
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran accused the United States on Saturday of violating their deal to end the Middle East war and hit US targets in the Gulf in retaliation for US strikes on Iranian territory. Israel, meanwhile, launched strikes in Lebanon and Hizbollah's leader Naim Qassem rejected a deal to end the conflict that has also threatened to derail the wider US-Iran peace effort. Iran said "these brutal attacks... are a blatant violation" of the deal to end the war, which began with US-Israeli strikes on Iran in late February and has sent oil prices surging as it disrupted the world economy.

items.title: Gazans flee scorching tents for a polluted sea
items.summary: RESIDENTS of the Gaza Strip have been flocking from suffocating tents to the territory's polluted Mediterranean shore to bathe and wash their clothes, as summer temperatures rise and fresh water remains hard to come by. Nearly all Gaza's population was displaced during two years of war between Israel and Hamas, with Gazans now crammed into a narrow strip along the coast, mainly in tents and damaged buildings. "The only outlet in the Gaza Strip, from north to south, is the sea," said Wadie al-Ras, 36, a displaced Palestinian standing on Gaza City's shore.

items.title: Expanded BudiMadani Diesel scheme kicks off
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia can now use their MyKad to buy subsidised diesel under the expanded BudiMadani Diesel scheme, with the price set at RM2.15 per litre starting yesterday before being reduced to RM2.10 on Wednesday. [[nid:1474609]] The scheme now covers company-owned pick-up trucks and sport utility vehicles (SUVs), and allows eligible recipients to transfer their diesel subsidy entitlement to immediate family members who are actual users of the vehicles. Registered diesel vehicle owners may also transfer their subsidy eligibility to an immediate family member who regularly uses the vehicle, subject to verification and eligibility requirements.

items.title: Records tumble as European heatwave moves east
items.summary: France is seeing "a higher than normal number of deaths" due to the heatwave baking the country, Health Minister Stephanie Rist said Saturday. The German Weather Service (DWD) issued a red alert for most of the country on Saturday, and said late afternoon that it had recorded its highest ever temperature with 41.5°C recorded in eastern Germany. Denmark's weather service said early afternoon Saturday that the country had recorded its highest temperature ever, and then an hour later said the record had lasted just an hour, with the mercury hitting 37°C in two places near Aarhus.

items.title: Venezuela quakes kill almost 1,500, with millions more in need
items.summary: LA GUAIRA, Venezuela: The death toll in Venezuela's twin earthquake disaster reached 1,430 Saturday, and millions more were feared to lack sanitation and other basic needs, as the first US aid flights landed in Caracas. Facing public outrage at the response by local officials, Venezuela's US-backed interim leader Delcy Rodriguez thanked other countries for the outpouring of aid. "It's just very chaotic, hot and unorganised," said Australian firefighter Craig Demeillon, 43, who traveled alone to La Guaira from Miami to help.

items.title: Argentina cabinet chief resigns after corruption allegations
items.summary: ARGENTINA: Argentine President Javier Milei's cabinet chief, Manuel Adorni, resigned on Saturday following a scandal that led to investigations into his spending in recent years. Adorni, who was appointed cabinet chief in November last year, is a close confidant of Milei, who initially named him as his spokesperson shortly after taking office in late 2023. "For the first time since Dec 10, 2023, I am going against your wishes," Adorni said in his resignation letter to Milei, which Adorni posted on X.

items.title: Hizbollah rejects deal with Israel, which expects 'extended stay' in Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Saturday declared his country's agreement with Lebanon "a blow to Iran and Hizbollah", while the fighter group's leader Naim Qassem declared it void. While Qassem attacked the deal, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz insisted that the country's troops would be able to stay in Lebanon so long as Hizbollah remained armed. He said he had instructed Israeli troops "to prepare for an extended stay" in southern Lebanon and that "there will be no redeployment by Israel in southern Lebanon, no withdrawal, as long as the terrorist organisation Hizbollah is not disarmed throughout Lebanon."

items.title: US, Iran clash, putting fragile deal under growing strain
items.summary: TEHERAN: A fragile truce between the United States and Iran hung in the balance Saturday after US forces said they struck Iranian targets in another tit-for-tat response to attacks on shipping in the vital Strait of Hormuz. US Central Command, or Centcom, said it hit "multiple targets in Iran." Iran said Saturday it had hit US targets in the Gulf in its own retaliation.

items.title: Johor polls: 'Main battle is between BN and PH' [WATCH]
items.summary: Of the total, 14 seats will see straight fights between BN and PH: 10 of them were held by BN and four were held by DAP. Twenty-seven seats will see three-cornered contests, and 12 will have four-cornered fights. "Many of these BN seats are straight fights with PH.

items.title: Temperature records shattered in Europe as deadly heatwave moves east
items.summary: COPENHAGEN: From Scandinavia to the Alps, Europeans endured sweltering conditions on Saturday as a heatwave linked to dozens of deaths spread east, shattering records with temperatures in some areas soaring above 40C (104F). Scientists said the stifling heatwave would have been virtually impossible without man-made climate change, which has made this week's night-time temperatures 100 times more likely than they would have been even two decades ago. "This heat isn't pleasant summer weather.

items.title: Newborn baby rescued from rubble of Venezuela quake
items.summary: VENEZUELA: A newborn baby has been rescued from a fallen building 32 hours after the twin earthquakes that devastated a Venezuelan coastal city. They carefully pass the baby, wrapped in a quilt, from person to person before cleaning the child gently with tissues, the video shows. The child's mother, was rescued an hour after the child.

items.title: 15 undocumented migrants nabbed in ops along Malaysia-Thailand border
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A total of 15 undocumented migrants, including five women, were arrested in a series of operations along the Malaysia-Thailand border here today. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the migrants, all from Myanmar, were detained after entering Malaysia illegally via the Golok River. He said the GOF later conducted four follow-up operations in the surrounding forest, resulting in the arrest of another 11 undocumented Myanmar nationals.

items.title: Saarani: Culture must drive economic growth, not just preserve heritage
items.summary: He said a nation's identity is shaped not only by its physical development but also by its culture, traditions and values passed down through generations. It is also known through its culture, the language spoken, the food served, the songs sung, the clothing worn and the values inherited from generation to generation," he said at the opening of the Malaysia Culture Festival 2026 at Bulatan Sultan Azlan Shah. Deputy Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Chiew Choon Man was also present.

items.title: 18 foreigners arrested in Kuching joint operation
items.summary: KUCHING: The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 18 foreigners during a joint operation with the Padawan Municipal Council (MPP) around Mile 10, Kota Padawan, yesterday. "A total of 26 foreigners were checked to verify their travel documents and immigration status. The suspects are believed to have committed offences under Section 15(1)(c) and Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, and Regulation 11(7)(a) of the Immigration Regulations 1959/63.

items.title: Russian hawks urge Putin to escalate war, drop US talks
items.summary: SEETHING over Ukrainian drone strikes and angered by what they see as a failed Unied States promise to broker an end to the war on favourable terms, Russian hardliners are urging President Vladimir Putin to abandon diplomacy and escalate. Nationalist voices have long pressed for full mobilisation, the destruction of Kyiv's government quarter, the assassination of President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and strikes on European drone factories. But Ukraine's deep strikes this month — targeting Moscow, St Petersburg and Crimea, as well as what Russia said were two deadly attacks on passenger buses — have sharpened and intensified those demands.

items.title: BBC shuts down long wave radio service after almost a century
items.summary: LONDON: The BBC has shut down its long wave radio service after almost 100 years, PA Media/dpa reported. BBC Radio 4, which was the last long wave radio station in the United Kingdom, had the service permanently switched off at 1am on Saturday. Long wave services have been used since 1934 and were used to transmit messages during the World War II.

items.title: Magnitude 6.1 quake hits Afghanistan
items.summary: KABUL: A 6.1-magnitude earthquake hit Afghanistan on Saturday, the United States Geological Survey said, with AFP journalists in the capital Kabul and elsewhere reporting strong shaking. The quake was widely felt by AFP journalists across multiple Afghan provinces, as well as in the Pakistani capital Islamabad. Journalists also felt the quake in Balkh and Badakhshan provinces, which border multiple countries including Tajikistan and Pakistan.

items.title: Labuan Airport reopens: WWII unexploded bombs safely disposed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Operations at Labuan Airport resumed as normal today after authorities safely disposed of three unexploded World War II bombs discovered near the runway. According to Berita RTM, the operation was launched after a contractor discovered an object believed to be a bomb while carrying out construction work on a hillside near the airport yesterday. Labuan police chief Superintendent Wan Mohd Firdaus Wan Zaki said inspections by the police Bomb Disposal Unit and the Royal Malaysian Air Force's (RMAF) Bomb Disposal Unit confirmed the object was a live aerial bomb.

items.title: Should we fear an AI bubble bust?
items.summary: RECENT swings in tech stocks are reviving fears of an AI bubble, and some experts warn that if it pops, the fallout could be bigger than anything Wall Street has ever seen. The five biggest companies on Wall Street — all tech giants — are worth a combined US$18 trillion, almost the size of China's economy. Tech stocks took a beating last week, reigniting old fears about an AI meltdown.

items.title: Johor election: Zahid pledges full support for federal unity government if BN wins
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 27 — BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has assured that he will ensure that the Johor State Government moves in line and provides full support to the federal government if it is given a mandate in the Johor state election. “I assure you that if the mandate is given again, the government in Johor state led by our friend will continue to work together with the central government for the benefit of the entire Johor people, God willing,” he said at the Johor State Kemas Staff Meeting at Pontian MARA Junior Science College here, today. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, also expressed his commitment to ensure full cooperation between the state administration and Putrajaya in order to strengthen the existing Unity Government.

items.title: Selangor religious dept denies mosque caused Ukay Perdana burial delay — blames family confusion and NGO
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) has denied allegations that a delay in the burial process at the Islamic Cemetery in Ukay Perdana, Hulu Klang, which went viral last Thursday, was caused by the Nurul Hidayah Mosque in Kampung Pandan Dalam. Jais director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad said the delay stemmed from confusion among the family of the deceased over burial arrangements, which also involved a non-governmental organisation (NGO), the Salatulrahim Welfare Organisation (BKS). Mohd Shahzihan explained that the deceased’s father later met the Assistant Manager of Nurul Hidayah Mosque at about 10.30 am to obtain the Islamic Burial Application Form required for burial at the cemetery and agreed for the mosque to take over funeral arrangements from BKS.

items.title: Jais raids Petaling Jaya premise over Shiite activities — 226 foreign nationals questioned
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) raided a premise at Jalan Pusat Perdagangan Lagoon Perdana in Petaling Jaya here, which was believed to be conducting activities contrary to fatwa and linked to Shiite teachings, two days ago. Jais director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad said the raid was conducted at 9.34 pm following public tip-offs, during which 226 foreign nationals comprising 147 men, 51 women and 28 children were inspected. Jais also advises Muslims not to be involved in any activities related to Shiite teachings,” he said.

items.title: Johor election key contests — Machap, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Kepong in spotlight
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Several high-profile contests are set to dominate the campaign for the 16th Johor state election, with heavyweight politicians, former ministers, and closely watched constituencies expected to shape the outcome when voters head to the polls on July 11. Topping the list is Machap, where Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi of Barisan Nasional (BN) faces a straight fight against Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Nur Hafiz Roslan, a lawyer. Another key battleground is Puteri Wangsa, where former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of PH faces the election’s highest-profile five-cornered contest against BN’s Teow Chia Ling, Nicholas Paul Vincent of Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), Rashifa Al Junied of the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA), and Independent Wang Wee Siong.

items.title: Malaysia to review diesel quota rates for commercial vehicles by August
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — The government will review the fixed quota rates of subsidised diesel for several types of land transport vehicles for goods under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) to ensure that the subsidy distribution is more accurate and in line with actual operational needs. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the decision was made following yesterday’s Cabinet Meeting which discussed the status of progress in implementing targeted fuel subsidy through the BUDI Diesel, BUDI95, Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) mechanisms. He said previously, the eligibility conditions for diesel subsidies were limited to vehicles registered in the name of individuals for BUDI Diesel and business vehicles or goods transport companies under the class or code of use for consumer goods transport for SKDS.

items.title: Australian woman assaulted in George Town cemetery, ex‑hotel guard jailed 12 years, caning
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 27 — A former hotel security guard was sentenced to 12 years’ imprisonment and 12 strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here after admitting to raping and committing unnatural sex on an Australian woman last year. The court handed down concurrent sentences after the accused changed his plea to guilty earlier this week, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. The accused was initially brought before the court on July 4 last year.

items.title: Dr Zaliha says former PKR members’ election bid under Bersama will not affect Pakatan campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The presence of several former PKR members contesting under the Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) ticket in the Johor state election (PRN) will not affect grassroots support for Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates, says Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa. She said PKR members remained united in facing challenges from all parties contesting the 56 Johor state seats. Among the former Johor PKR members contesting under the Bersama ticket are Stanley Tan in the Stulang state seat, who previously served at the service centre of Johor Bahru Member of Parliament Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, and Dr Zamil Najwah, PKR’s candidate for the Larkin state seat in the 2022 Johor election.

items.title: Pakatan ditches ‘idealistic’ hundred-page manifestos for ‘realistic’ Johor vows; keeping MB choice under wraps
items.summary: PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the coalition’s manifesto has been finalised and is awaiting final endorsement by its top leadership at a meeting on June 29 before being launched, Harian Metro reported this afternoon. Saifuddin Nasution said this time the coalition wanted to present a manifesto that was “realistic, tangible and reasonable”, with a simpler presentation that would not dilute its policy offerings. On the question of PH's menteri besar candidate should the coalition win the Johor state election, Saifuddin Nasution said an announcement would only be made when the time was right.

items.title: Orang Asli party ASLI makes election debut with Endau seat bid in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) has made its first election appearance by fielding a candidate for the Endau state seat in the 16th Johor state election. The Endau seat, with 28,767 registered voters, will see a four-cornered fight involving Jati, Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Alwiyah Talib, Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Saiful Nizam Samat and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Hasnul Hakimi Hussien. Jati, 52, an Orang Asli from Endau, said ASLI’s participation aims to elevate the voice of the community, particularly in state leadership.

items.title: Anwar urges Pakatan candidates to win Johor voters’ trust, prove civil politics can prevail
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today wished all PH candidates contesting the 16th Johor state election a successful campaign. Nomination for the 16th Johor state election concluded today, with 172 candidates confirmed to contest the 56 seats. PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) are fielding 56 candidates each, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 33, Parti Bersama Malaysia with 15, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) with four, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) with one candidate each, while six candidates are contesting as Independents.

items.title: Anwar to join Johor election campaign with Pakatan grand finale two days before polling, says Saifuddin Nasution
items.summary: KULAI, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to hit the campaign trail in Johor, with a grand finale Pakatan Harapan (PH) programme scheduled for July 9, just two days before Johoreans head to the polls. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail confirmed the news, saying the Prime Minister will be lending his weight to the PH campaign. The PH grand finale on July 9 will also see all component party presidents in attendance, Saifuddin revealed.

items.title: ‘Competing in the daytime but friends at night’, Mat Sabu says Pakatan‑BN clash confined to Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The contest between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor state election does not in any way disrupt the administration of the federal unity government, said Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu. He said that since the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly, relations and duties, particularly those involving Cabinet ministers from both parties, have continued smoothly. The Amanah president said that although both sides have their own strategies and calculations at the state level to win the election, this does not affect stability at the federal level.

items.title: Anwar says Bandar Malaysia Malay Reserve Land move requires political courage to protect Bumiputera interests
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the decision to gazette 50 acres (20.23 hectares) of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land is a significant step to safeguard Bumiputera interests, requiring both political courage and careful consensus-building. He said the 480-acre Bandar Malaysia site was taken back by the government after the project failed due to alleged abuses under the previous administration, and is now being managed by Khazanah Nasional Bhd and Petroliam Nasional Bhd. “The 50 acres is equivalent to 5,000 acres in Kedah in terms of value.

items.title: DUKE highway crash: Police confirm two students burnt to death in BMW blaze, survivor hospitalised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 27 — Two university students were burnt to death in a crash involving a car at the Jalan Kuching exit ramp on the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) here early this morning, while another passenger survived with minor injuries. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said preliminary investigations found that the BMW 328i Sport was travelling from Jalan Duta towards Selayang when it was believed to have been speeding before the driver lost control, skidded, and crashed into the left road divider, causing the vehicle to catch fire. He said the victims were two Malaysian students — the 22-year-old male driver, a student at Queensland University of Technology, Australia, and the 21-year-old male front passenger, a Sunway University student.

items.title: BN confident of winning back Bukit Kepong seat as Johor election campaign begin
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has kicked off its campaign for the 16th Johor state election with high confidence in its bid to wrest back the Bukit Kepong seat from Perikatan Nasional (PN). Bukit Kepong, which falls under the Pagoh parliamentary constituency, has 37,683 eligible voters for this state election. The Johor state election will take place on July 11, with early voting on July 7.

items.title: Anwar announces RM1.5m allocation for Syariah legal aid, says reforms will not affect non-Muslims’ rights
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has announced a RM1.5 million allocation to strengthen access to Syariah legal aid services for the underprivileged, alongside renewed assurances that efforts to enhance the Syariah judicial system will not affect the rights of non-Muslims. He said the allocation to the Syariah Lawyers Association of Malaysia would be channelled through the Syariah Legal Aid Centre (BAGUS) to expand access to representation for those unable to afford Syariah legal services, particularly the poor and single mothers. “We have good laws, good courts, good lawyers and good judges, but access remains an issue.

items.title: In Malaysia, one in four rehab patients now battling addiction and mental illness
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, June 27 — More than one in four drug abusers undergoing rehabilitation in Malaysia this year have also been diagnosed with mental health disorders, as the rise of synthetic drugs reshapes the country's addiction crisis. National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 substance abusers undergoing treatment at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres between January and March this year were found to have what is known as a dual diagnosis, the New Straits Times reported today. Ruslin said the trend showed drug rehabilitation was no longer just about treating addiction, as an increasing number of patients were also struggling with mental illness.

items.title: Malaysia's biggest tech challenge is talent, not infrastructure, says Mosti
items.summary: Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation’s (MOSTI) deputy secretary-general (Planning and Science Enculturation) Datuk Ruziah Shafie said that developing a stronger, more resilient, and globally competitive future workforce requires early exposure to science, technology, and innovation (STI), as well as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), through continuous science culture and outreach programmes. She said that the National Science Week (MSN) programme is one of MOSTI’s key initiatives to cultivate youth’s interest in these fields while also bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real needs of high-technology industries. Malaysia can no longer remain merely a consumer of technology; we must rise to become a creator of technology.

items.title: Prescription for trouble: Unlicensed doctor at Subang Bestari clinic nabbed after eight months of cut-price consultations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — A Pakistani man suspected of posing as an unlicensed doctor has been detained after Immigration officers raided a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, uncovering what authorities believe was an illegally operated medical practice that had been running for eight months. The suspect is believed to have been working as an “unlicensed doctor” despite not possessing any valid pass or travel documents to remain in Malaysia, the Immigration Department said on Facebook today. The private clinic was allegedly run by a foreign national who did not have legal authorisation to work in Malaysia’s medical sector, the department said.

items.title: Johor polls: Maszlee pledges balanced development across all districts
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state constituency, Dr Maszlee Malik, is determined to bring balanced development across all districts in Johor if given the mandate in the upcoming polls. He said a PH victory in Puteri Wangsa, and across the state, is crucial to usher in a new chapter of development that is not concentrated solely in southern Johor, but spread evenly so that every segment of society can enjoy a better quality of life. “We want Johor to progress in a balanced way across all its districts, not just the southern part.

items.title: Johor polls: Zahid pledges ‘mature politics’, says BN won’t attack rivals
items.summary: PONTIAN, June 27 — BN will adopt a ‘mature politics’ approach by refraining from attacking, criticising or disparaging its rivals throughout the campaign for the 16th Johor state election. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach is important to preserve political harmony, while allowing the coalition’s campaign machinery to focus on promoting the strengths of its candidates courteously and constructively. In the 2022 Johor state election, BN secured a two-thirds majority after winning 40 of the 56 DUN seats to form the state government.

items.title: Police net two suspects, seize suspected heroin after Jalan Kuching pursuit that led to pile-up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Two men suspected of transporting heroin were arrested after a high-speed police chase ended in a multi-vehicle collision on Jalan Kuching, with officers recovering nearly 20g of suspected drugs from their car. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects, aged 40 and 42, have been remanded to assist investigations, the New Straits Times reported this afternoon. Fadil said police instructed the driver to stop, but the vehicle instead sped off, triggering a pursuit towards Jalan Kuching.

items.title: Johor polls: 172 candidates confirmed after nomination closes
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — The Election Commission (EC) has confirmed that 172 candidates from various political parties will contest the 16th Johor state election after the nomination process concluded at all 56 nomination centres today. Ramlan said 14 seats would see straight fights, with 27 featuring three-cornered contests, 12 involving four candidates and three drawing five candidates. He said the campaign period officially begins today and will run until 11.59 pm on July 10, with 56 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams (PP-KPR) deployed to monitor all campaign activities.

items.title: In Pagoh, Muhyiddin shrugs off PAS snub, vows Bersatu machinery will drive Johor fight
items.summary: PAGOH, June 27 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) founding president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said he respected PAS’ decision not to assist the party’s campaign in the 16th Johor state election, stressing that Bersatu would press ahead on the strength of its own machinery. He said he believed the decision would not affect Bersatu’s campaign, which remained focused on the party’s policies and track record in winning public support. Muhyiddin said Bersatu would nevertheless remain open to assisting its coalition partners if its support was needed during the Johor election campaign.

items.title: PM Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow, 20 new stations set to transform commutes
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 27 — The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is now ready for passenger service. “The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato’ Menteri, and UiTM Shah Alam. “The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi, and Johan Setia,” he told reporters during a recent pre-opening media ride on the new service.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz installs new Undangs of Sungei Ujong and Rembau, calls for loyalty
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 27 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today formalised the installation of two new Undangs (ruling chieftains) in separate ceremonies at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, underscoring the continued importance of adat and consensus in the state’s governance structure. In the morning ceremony, Muhammad Faris Johari, 28, was installed as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, bearing the title Datuk Klana Petra, while in the afternoon, Hassan Ab Hamid, 67, was installed as the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, with the title Datuk Sedia Raja. Also in attendance were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Panglima Besar Tunku Zain Al-‘Abidin.
